title: World of Fear & Hunger
text: Fear & Hunger is set in a dark fantasy world where the dungeons of fear and hunger hold ancient secrets, monstrous creatures, and the promise of salvation or damnation. The world is brutal, with no leveling system, permanent death, and survival depending on limbs, items, and knowledge. The character Le'garde is prophesied to descend into the dungeons seeking the Throne of Ascension.

title: Coin Flip Mechanic
text: Many enemies in Fear & Hunger have a coin flip attack — an unavoidable instant-kill or massive-damage attack on certain turns. The player must Guard on the coin flip turn to survive. Destroying the limb that enables the coin flip (e.g., the Guard's right arm for Mad Rush) disables the coin flip permanently. Coin flips are a core mechanic and failing them can end a run.

title: No Leveling System
text: Fear & Hunger has no experience points or leveling system. Character stats can only be increased through equipment, items (such as Blue vials), or by interacting with specific entities like the Human Hydra using a Ring of Wraiths. This makes each character's base stats critical and equipment choices life-or-death decisions.

title: Saving and Death
text: The game can only be saved at ritual circles, which are scattered throughout the dungeons. Death is permanent — there is no automatic checkpointing. Players must weigh the risk of pressing forward against the safety of backtracking to save. Some ritual circles are hidden or require specific conditions to access.

title: Limb Targeting System
text: Fear & Hunger features a limb-based combat system where enemies have multiple targetable limbs, each with its own HP pool and attacks. Destroying limbs changes enemy behavior — removing a cleaver arm prevents cleaver attacks, destroying a stinger disables thrust attacks, and destroying legs reduces mobility. Understanding limb priorities is essential for survival. Limbs lost by party members are permanently gone unless special restorative items are found.

title: Hunger Mechanic
text: Hunger is a persistent mechanic in Fear & Hunger. Characters must eat food items regularly or suffer increasing penalties. Starvation leads to reduced stats and eventually death. Food can be scavenged from the environment, dropped by enemies, or purchased from merchants. Some food items, like rotten meat, can cause infection or poisoning.

title: Infection and Bleeding
text: Infection is a dangerous status effect that worsens over time if untreated. Green herbs can cure infection. Bleeding causes HP loss each turn and must be treated with cloth or bandages, or it will eventually kill the character. Both conditions are especially dangerous during prolonged dungeon exploration where resources are scarce.

title: Mind and Sanity
text: Mind represents a character's mental stability. Low mind can cause phobias to activate, hallucinations, and negative combat effects. Mind is restored by consuming certain items like Tobacco or reading books, or by resting in safe areas. Sanity management is as important as physical health in the dungeons.
